stock_a1200.label: Amiga 1200 (stock)
stock_a1200.notes: Base A1200 with 2MB Chip RAM only. Limited stack headroom.

a1200_accel.label: Amiga 1200 + Accelerator
a1200_accel.notes: A1200 with 68030 accelerator (e.g. Blizzard 1230) and 8MB Fast RAM

candidates.name: basename
candidates.description: Strip directory and suffix from filenames
candidates.notes: Trivial single-file utility, no POSIX deps beyond stdio
candidates.aminet_notes: ARPClones-37.1 (1995) and BP102 (1992) provide Amiga variants. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes POSIX-compliant basename.

candidates.name: dirname
candidates.notes: Trivial single-file utility
candidates.aminet_notes: Not available as standalone utility. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes it.

candidates.name: tr
candidates.notes: Multi-file (tr.c, str.c, extern.h); pure stdio + getopt. No locale/wchar deps.
candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: fmt
candidates.notes: Text formatter. Locale/wchar paths can be #ifdef-d out for ASCII-only operation.

analysis.blocker_workaround: #ifdef __AMIGA__ to skip locale/wchar paths; ASCII-only is fine for Amiga

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: fold
candidates.notes: Simple line folder. Locale/wchar for multibyte chars can be #ifdef-d out.

analysis.blocker_workaround: #ifdef __AMIGA__ to skip multibyte paths; ASCII fold works fine

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: rev
candidates.notes: Trivial single-file, ~50 lines
candidates.aminet_notes: AlterASCII (1997) provides text reversal/transformation, but differs in purpose from POSIX rev. Not a direct port.

candidates.name: column
candidates.notes: Columnate lists. ioctl only for terminal width detection (easy workaround). Locale paths can be skipped.

analysis.blocker_workaround: ioctl(TIOCGWINSZ) replaced with getenv(COLUMNS) or default 80; locale #ifdef-d out

candidates.aminet_notes: Not found on Aminet. Not in GNU coreutils. NICHE: BSD column(1) may exist separately, but not located. HIGH PORTING VALUE.

candidates.name: expand
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio
candidates.aminet_notes: Expand.lha (1992) provides 'TABs to spaces and vice versa'—older standalone implementation.

candidates.name: unexpand
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio
candidates.aminet_notes: Not found on Aminet. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes it.

candidates.name: paste
candidates.description: Merge lines of files side by side
candidates.notes: Merge lines of files. Pure stdio + getopt + getline. No OS deps.
candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: join
candidates.description: Relational database join on sorted files
candidates.notes: Relational join. Locale/wchar paths for multibyte field separators can be #ifdef-d out.

analysis.blocker_workaround: #ifdef __AMIGA__ to skip locale/wchar; ASCII join works correctly

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: comm
candidates.description: Select or reject lines common to two files
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio
candidates.aminet_notes: Not available as standalone utility. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes it.

candidates.name: tsort
candidates.notes: Topological sort. Pure algorithm + stdio. No OS deps. Larger due to cycle detection.
candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: ls
candidates.notes: Complex multi-file (ls.c, print.c, cmp.c, util.c, extern.h, ls.h). Needs fts replacement or shim. User/group display needs stubs. Significant effort.

analysis.blocker_workaround: fts can be replaced with opendir/readdir recursive walk; user/group stubs return "amiga"; readlink returns -1; ioctl(TIOCGWINSZ) use COLUMNS env

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: file
candidates.description: Determine file type via magic numbers
candidates.notes: Multi-file with built-in magic detection (10 files, ~3700 LOC). fork/wait for sandbox can be bypassed. Complex but self-contained (no libmagic dep).

analysis.blocker_workaround: fork/wait used for sandboxed magic testing — can run unsandboxed on AmigaOS (no privilege separation needed). getpw*/getuid stubs. readlink returns -1.

candidates.aminet_notes: Not found on Aminet. GNU file utility exists but not ported. MODERATE PORTING VALUE (niche use case).

candidates.name: du
candidates.notes: Disk usage. Core logic is simple but depends on fts for directory traversal. Rewrite with opendir/readdir is straightforward.

analysis.blocker_workaround: fts can be replaced with opendir/readdir recursive walk + stat for sizes

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: find
candidates.notes: Complex multi-file (7 files, ~2800 LOC). -exec needs fork/exec (blocker). -user/-group need getpw/getgr. fts needs replacement. Partial port feasible (find without -exec).

analysis.blocker_workaround: -exec requires fork/exec (Tier 3 blocker). Subset without -exec/-user/-group is feasible with fts replacement.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU findutils 4.1 (m68k, 1998). Note: very old version (1998), missing modern features (UPGRADE candidate).

candidates.name: xargs
candidates.description: Build and execute command lines from stdin
candidates.notes: Fundamentally depends on fork+exec to run child processes. AmigaDOS System() could work but is a significant rewrite.

analysis.blocker_workaround: Core function is fork+exec — could use AmigaDOS SystemTags() but would be a major rewrite

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU findutils 4.1 (m68k, 1998). Note: very old version (1998), missing modern features (UPGRADE candidate).

candidates.name: touch
candidates.description: Change file access and modification times
candidates.notes: Change file timestamps. Needs utimensat/futimens shim (maps to SetFileDate). Otherwise clean.

analysis.blocker_workaround: utimensat/futimens can be shimmed via AmigaOS SetFileDate() — straightforward Tier 2 shim

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: ln
candidates.notes: Create links. symlink() is a hard blocker (no AmigaOS equivalent). Hard link via AmigaOS MakeLink() is possible for -f mode. ln -s would fail gracefully.

analysis.blocker_workaround: AmigaOS has no symlinks (only MakeLink for hard links on FFS). Hard link only mode feasible.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: cp
candidates.notes: Copy files. Multi-file (cp.c, utils.c). Needs fts replacement. Ownership/symlink operations need stubs. Recursive copy feasible with workarounds.

analysis.blocker_workaround: fts replaceable with opendir/readdir. fchmod/fchown/lchown can be no-ops. symlink/readlink: skip -R symlink preservation. Major effort.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: mv
candidates.notes: Move/rename files. Calls cpmain/rmmain directly for cross-device (no fork). fchmod/fchown are trivial no-op stubs. Feasible.

analysis.blocker_workaround: fchmod/fchown can be no-op stubs — AmigaOS has limited permission model. Uses cpmain/rmmain directly (no fork).

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: rm
candidates.notes: Remove files. fts needed only for -r (recursive). Simple rm without -r is trivial. fts replacement for -r is moderate effort.

analysis.blocker_workaround: fts can be replaced with opendir/readdir recursive walk for -r mode. Non-recursive rm needs no fts.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: mkdir
candidates.notes: Create directories. Pure shim (mkdir, chmod, stat). No blockers.
candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: rmdir
candidates.notes: Remove empty directories. Trivial — just rmdir() + getopt + err.
candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: chmod
candidates.notes: Change file modes. fts only for -R. AmigaOS has limited permission bits but chmod shim exists. Non-recursive mode is clean.

analysis.blocker_workaround: fts needed for -R (recursive). Non-recursive chmod is simple (just chmod() shim). fts replaceable with opendir/readdir.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: env
candidates.notes: Run program in modified environment. Core use (env VAR=val cmd) needs execvp. env without command (just print) works.

analysis.blocker_workaround: execvp can be replaced with AmigaDOS SystemTags() for running the command — moderate rewrite of exec path

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: printenv
candidates.description: Print all or part of environment
candidates.notes: Print environment variables. Trivial — uses extern environ + getenv. Maps to AmigaOS GetVar/ScanVars.
candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: uname
candidates.notes: System identification. uname() syscall needs shim returning AmigaOS identity. Straightforward.

analysis.blocker_workaround: uname() syscall shimmed to return static AmigaOS values (sysname=AmigaOS, machine=m68k, etc.)

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: hostname
candidates.notes: Get/set hostname. gethostname available via bsdsocket.library or env var. sethostname less useful on Amiga.

analysis.blocker_workaround: gethostname via AmigaOS GetVar("hostname",...) or bsdsocket.library gethostname(). sethostname can be a stub or SetVar.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: id
candidates.notes: Print user/group IDs. Fundamentally Unix user model. Can stub everything to return single-user values. Useful for scripts expecting id output.

analysis.blocker_workaround: All user/group functions need stubs. AmigaOS is single-user: uid=0, gid=0, user="amiga". Feasible but many stubs.

candidates.aminet_notes: GNU coreutils 5.2.1-9 (m68k, 2010). Note: version is from 2005, 20+ years old but functional.

candidates.name: whoami
candidates.notes: Print effective user name. Symlink to id on OpenBSD (same binary). Same stubs as id needed.

analysis.blocker_workaround: Same as id — whoami is a symlink to id on OpenBSD. Stub user functions to return "amiga".

candidates.aminet_notes: No dedicated Aminet package found. Available in Geek Gadgets as part of GNU coreutils. AmigaOS 3.x lacks native equivalent.

candidates.name: logname
candidates.notes: Print login name. Just getlogin() + puts. Trivial with stub.

analysis.blocker_workaround: getlogin() stubbed to return "amiga" — AmigaOS is single-user

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Part of Geek Gadgets/coreutils. AmigaOS has no direct equivalent (uses $LOGNAME env var when set).

candidates.name: tty
candidates.description: Print name of terminal on stdin
candidates.notes: Print terminal name. Just ttyname() + isatty. Trivial with stub.

analysis.blocker_workaround: ttyname() stubbed to return "CON:" or "*" — AmigaOS console device name

candidates.aminet_notes: No standalone Aminet package found. Standard Unix utility; AmigaDOS uses different device model (CON: vs /dev/tty).

candidates.name: echo
candidates.notes: Trivial ~30 lines; no POSIX deps
candidates.aminet_notes: Not found as standalone. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes POSIX echo. AlterASCII (1997) exists but is unrelated reversal tool.

candidates.name: printf
candidates.notes: Format and print data. Pure C implementation of printf format strings. No OS deps beyond stdio.
candidates.aminet_notes: Available as amiga.lib/printf() function in AmigaOS SDK, but shell command printf likely missing. Geek Gadgets has GNU printf.

candidates.name: nl
candidates.notes: Number lines. Uses regex (implemented in posix-emu). setlocale is trivial stub. Clean port.

analysis.blocker_workaround: setlocale() is a no-op on AmigaOS — stub to return "C"

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. GNU coreutils line numbering utility. Available via Geek Gadgets.

candidates.name: colrm
candidates.notes: Single file, pure stdio + wchar; setlocale/mbtowc/wcwidth need stubs or removal

analysis.blocker_workaround: setlocale/mbtowc/wcwidth can be stubbed as no-ops (single-byte locale only); remove wchar path

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. BSD/GNU utility. Not part of standard Amiga distribution.

candidates.name: cat
candidates.notes: Simple single-file; uses open/read/write/fstat for raw mode, pledge/getprogname for BSD; straightforward port

analysis.blocker_workaround: getprogname replaced with __progname

candidates.aminet_notes: AmigaDOS TYPE command is the native equivalent. Unix-style cat may exist in Geek Gadgets; not confirmed on standalone Aminet.

candidates.name: true
candidates.notes: Trivial; one line of C
candidates.aminet_notes: Not available as standalone utility. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes it.

candidates.name: false
candidates.notes: Trivial; exit(RETURN_ERROR) for Amiga shell compatibility
candidates.aminet_notes: Not available as standalone utility. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes it.

candidates.name: sleep
candidates.notes: Trivial; uses amiport_sleep shim

analysis.blocker_workaround: nanosleep can be replaced with amiport_sleep() for whole seconds or Delay() for sub-second; getprogname replaced with __progname

candidates.aminet_notes: wsz13 (1993) is a window mover/sizer with sleep feature—not a standalone sleep utility. GNU coreutils-bin v5.2.1-9 (2006) includes POSIX sleep.

candidates.name: date
candidates.description: Display or set date and time
candidates.notes: Uses time/strftime/strptime/localtime/mktime (libnix has these); adjtime/settimeofday/logwtmp/syslog/getlogin are set-mode only (stub); epoch offset needed

analysis.blocker_workaround: adjtime/settimeofday/logwtmp/syslog/getlogin only used for set-time mode (stub as no-ops returning error); strptime bundleable; timegm = mktime with TZ trick; strtonum replaceable with strtol

candidates.aminet_notes: AmigaDOS DATE command exists (adate.lha also available on Aminet). Unix date format differs from AmigaDOS.

candidates.name: expr
candidates.notes: Uses regex (Tier 2 implemented) for : operator; strtonum/asprintf/strcoll replaceable; pure computation

analysis.blocker_workaround: strtonum replaceable with strtol wrapper; strcoll replaceable with strcmp (no locale on Amiga)

candidates.aminet_notes: No standalone Aminet package found. Part of Geek Gadgets. AmigaOS lacks native shell arithmetic.

candidates.name: test
candidates.notes: Uses stat/lstat/access/isatty (all Tier 1); geteuid/getegid stub as 0; strtonum/strlcpy/timespeccmp available; pure logic

analysis.blocker_workaround: geteuid/getegid stub as returning 0 (single-user AmigaOS); strtonum replaceable with strtol

candidates.aminet_notes: AmigaDOS has IF conditional syntax. Unix test/[ builtin not found standalone on Aminet. Available via Geek Gadgets.

candidates.name: seq
candidates.notes: OpenBSD has standalone seq (separate from jot); uses getopt_long/asprintf/localeconv; pure math + formatting

analysis.blocker_workaround: localeconv can return stub struct with '.' as decimal point

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. GNU utility for generating number sequences. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: jot
candidates.notes: Uses arc4random/arc4random_uniform (replace with rand/srand); strtonum/asprintf; pure math

analysis.blocker_workaround: arc4random/arc4random_uniform replaced with rand()/srand(); strtonum replaceable with strtol

candidates.aminet_notes: BSD utility. No Aminet package found. Not in standard Amiga distribution.

candidates.name: factor
candidates.description: Factor a number and print prime factors
candidates.notes: Pure math (prime factorization); in OpenBSD games/ not usr.bin/; uses strtoull, fgets, pledge; no POSIX deps
candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Niche mathematical utility. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: which
candidates.description: Locate a program file in the user's path
candidates.notes: Scans PATH for executables; uses getenv/stat/access (Tier 1); setgid/setuid/getegid/geteuid stub; strsep available in libnix

analysis.blocker_workaround: setgid/setuid/geteuid/getegid stub as no-ops (single-user AmigaOS); warnc replaceable with warn; PATH maps to C: directory scanning

candidates.aminet_notes: No standalone Aminet package found. AmigaDOS uses PATH: equivalent; "which" functionality not standard. Available via Geek Gadgets.

candidates.name: nohup
candidates.notes: Uses execvp (Tier 3 blocker); also signal/isatty/dup2/open/lseek/strlcpy/strlcat; concept maps poorly to AmigaOS (no SIGHUP)

analysis.blocker_workaround: execvp could be replaced with SystemTagList() to launch command, but nohup semantics (ignore SIGHUP) are meaningless on AmigaOS — no SIGHUP exists

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Unix job control utility. AmigaDOS Run command provides similar "detach" functionality.

candidates.name: look
candidates.description: Display lines beginning with a given string
candidates.notes: Binary search on sorted file using mmap (Tier 2 implemented); needs substitute for /usr/share/dict/words; uses open/fstat/mmap

analysis.blocker_workaround: errc replaceable with err; default dictionary path changed to S:words or LOCALE:words

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. BSD word lookup utility. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: strings
candidates.notes: NOT a standalone OpenBSD utility — strings is from binutils/ELF toolchain. Need alternative source (FreeBSD or sbase). Analysis deferred.
candidates.aminet_notes: No standalone Aminet package found. Binary analysis tool. Not in standard Amiga distribution.

candidates.name: cmp
candidates.description: Compare two files byte by byte
candidates.notes: Multi-file (cmp.c + regular.c + special.c + misc.c); uses open/fstat/mmap (all implemented); pure file comparison
candidates.aminet_notes: No standalone Aminet package found. File comparison tool. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: cksum
candidates.description: Display file checksums and block counts
candidates.notes: NOT a standalone OpenBSD utility — cksum is handled by bin/md5 on OpenBSD. FreeBSD has standalone cksum (~95 LOC, simple CRC32/sum). Could port FreeBSD version.
candidates.aminet_notes: No standalone Aminet package found. Checksum utility. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: md5
candidates.notes: Handles MD5/SHA1/SHA256/SHA512/RMD160/CKSUM; depends on libc hash implementations (MD5_*, SHA256_*, etc.) which are NOT in libnix — need to bundle. Uses getline/getrusage.

analysis.blocker_workaround: getrusage stub as returning zeros (timing mode only); MD5/SHA hash implementations must be bundled from libressl-portable or public domain sources

candidates.aminet_notes: MD5SUM.lha (util/crypt), md5sum2.lha (util/misc), ARexxMD5.lha, and md5.lha all available on Aminet. Multiple implementations.

candidates.name: pr
candidates.notes: Complex text formatter; uses signal/sigaction (Tier 2 not implemented)/sigprocmask, time/localtime/strftime, isatty, fstat, vasprintf, strtonum. Signal handling is for SIGINT during pagination.

analysis.blocker_workaround: sigaction/sigprocmask replaced with signal() shim for basic SIGINT handling; strtonum replaceable with strtol

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Print file formatter. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: col
candidates.description: Filter reverse line feeds from input
candidates.notes: Single file, pure character processing; uses reallocarray (Tier 1), strtonum, getopt, err/errx/warnx; no file I/O beyond stdio

analysis.blocker_workaround: strtonum replaceable with strtol wrapper

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Filter for reverse line feeds. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: vis
candidates.notes: Small utility but depends on vis(3) library function from libc — NOT in libnix. Need to bundle vis.c from OpenBSD libc.

analysis.blocker_workaround: vis(3) function can be bundled from OpenBSD libc (single file, ~200 LOC, ISC license); strtonum replaceable with strtol

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Display non-printing characters (BSD utility). Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: rs
candidates.notes: Matrix reshape; uses getline/reallocarray (Tier 1), strtonum, setlocale; pure stdio + malloc

analysis.blocker_workaround: strtonum replaceable with strtol; setlocale stub as no-op

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Reshape tool for data manipulation. Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: dc
candidates.notes: Arbitrary precision calculator (5 .c files + 3 .h); DEPENDS ON OpenSSL BN_* (BIGNUM) for arbitrary precision — major external dependency. Uses signal, reallocarray, err/errx/warn/warnx, strlcpy, fstat, asprintf.

analysis.blocker_workaround: setproctitle stubbed as no-op

candidates.aminet_notes: No Aminet package found. Desk calculator (arbitrary precision). Not in standard Amiga tools.

candidates.name: apply
candidates.notes: Uses fork/exec/waitpid/signal/sigprocmask -- Tier 3 blockers (no fork on AmigaOS). Could theoretically use SystemTagList but would need full rewrite of exec logic.

analysis.blocker_workaround: Rewrite to use SystemTagList() instead of fork/exec/wait -- major restructure

candidates.aminet_notes: No apply tool found on Aminet. Typical POSIX shell utility for applying commands to argument lists.

candidates.name: awk
candidates.notes: Plan 9 awk (BWK). Uses popen/pclose for pipes and system() for awk system(). Plan9-specific includes (u.h, libc.h, bio.h, utf.h) in re.c need porting. ~4600 LOC across 10 files. popen is not implemented.

candidates.name: getopt
candidates.notes: Trivial wrapper around getopt(3). pledge is no-op. Only uses stdio, getopt, err, exit.
candidates.aminet_notes: getopt_long.lha exists but is a C library package (dev/c), not a CLI tool. No standalone getopt CLI found.

candidates.name: seq
candidates.notes: Single-file sbase seq. Uses util.h (sbase utility library -- eprintf, ARGBEGIN macros, fshut). Pure stdio + math. Needs sbase util.c bundled or reimplemented.

candidates.name: tac
candidates.description: Concatenate files in reverse line order
candidates.notes: File tac.c does NOT exist in sbase (checked michaelforney/sbase and suckless.org). sbase has tail.c but no tac. Candidate should be removed or re-sourced.

candidates.name: sponge
candidates.description: Soak up stdin and write to file
candidates.notes: Extremely minimal (37 LOC). Uses mkstemp, unlink, lseek, creat + sbase util.h. All POSIX calls are Tier 1 shimmed. /tmp path needs T: replacement.

candidates.name: install
candidates.notes: Uses grp.h/pwd.h (getgrnam/getpwnam -- no users/groups on AmigaOS), lchown (no-op ok), symlink (no symlinks on AmigaOS), sys/wait.h, mknod. Core copy functionality is portable but ownership/permissions features are no-ops on AmigaOS.

candidates.name: unicode
candidates.notes: Plan 9 unicode lookup tool. Uses Plan9-specific includes (u.h, libc.h, bio.h) and Plan9 types (Rune, Biobuf). Needs Plan9 compat shim or rewrite of I/O to stdio. Pure computation otherwise.

candidates.name: freq
candidates.notes: Plan 9 character frequency counter. Uses Plan9 includes (u.h, libc.h, bio.h). Large static array (Runemax+1 uvlong = ~8MB for full Unicode). Needs Plan9 compat shim. Could be rewritten to use stdio easily.

candidates.name: ascii
candidates.notes: Plan 9 ASCII reference tool. Uses Plan9 includes (u.h, libc.h, bio.h). Pure computation + formatted output. Could be rewritten to stdio.

candidates.name: hoc
candidates.notes: Plan 9 calculator language (K&R hoc). Uses Plan9 includes (u.h, libc.h, bio.h), yacc grammar, floating point math. ~1263 LOC across 5 files. Needs Plan9 compat + yacc build step. No fork/exec/popen.

candidates.name: uuencode
candidates.notes: Single-file uuencode with base64 mode (-m). Uses fstat, fread, fwrite, fopen, stdio. All calls are Tier 1. Needs sbase util.c bundled.

candidates.name: uudecode
candidates.notes: Single-file uudecode with base64 support. Uses lstat, fopen, chmod, getline, stdio. All Tier 1. Needs sbase util.c bundled.

candidates.name: base64
candidates.notes: File base64.c does NOT exist in sbase (checked michaelforney/sbase and suckless.org). sbase has uuencode with -m base64 mode instead. Candidate should be removed or re-sourced.

candidates.name: sha256sum
candidates.notes: Tiny wrapper (47 LOC) around bundled SHA-256 implementation via crypt.h/sha256.h. Pure C, no OS deps beyond stdio. Needs sbase crypt.c + sha256.c bundled.

candidates.name: md5sum
candidates.notes: Tiny wrapper (47 LOC) around bundled MD5 implementation via crypt.h/md5.h. Pure C, no OS deps beyond stdio. Needs sbase crypt.c + md5.c bundled.

candidates.name: xd
candidates.notes: File xd.c does NOT exist in sbase (checked michaelforney/sbase and suckless.org). sbase has od.c instead. Candidate should be removed or re-sourced.

candidates.name: split
candidates.notes: Single-file split utility (92 LOC). Uses fopen, fclose, getc, putc, strlen, strcmp. Pure stdio. Needs sbase util.c bundled.

candidates.name: od
candidates.notes: Single-file octal dump (323 LOC). Uses open/read/close from fcntl.h/unistd.h, malloc/free, queue.h macros. All POSIX calls are Tier 1 shimmed. Needs sbase util.c + queue.h bundled.

candidates.name: sha1sum
candidates.notes: Tiny wrapper (47 LOC) around bundled SHA-1 implementation via crypt.h/sha1.h. Pure C, no OS deps beyond stdio. Needs sbase crypt.c + sha1.c bundled.

candidates.name: awk-plan9
candidates.notes: Research-only. processing utility from 9base project.
candidates.aminet_notes: Plan 9 AWK variant not found on Aminet. Only ATT-awk-1_0 (traditional AT&T AWK) exists.

candidates.name: sbase-seq
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: seq utility (sbase implementation) not found on Aminet.

candidates.name: sbase-sponge
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: sponge utility (from moreutils) not found on Aminet. sbase does not include sponge.

candidates.name: sbase-xinstall
candidates.notes: Research-only. system_admin utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: xinstall.lha exists at https://aminet.net/package/util/boot/xinstall. Amiga-native install utility, not sbase variant. Different purpose from sbase-xinstall.

candidates.name: 9base-unicode
candidates.notes: Research-only. development utility from 9base project.
candidates.aminet_notes: 9base unicode tools not found. Unicode support packages exist but not specifically 9base.

candidates.name: 9base-freq
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from 9base project.
candidates.aminet_notes: freq tool (9base implementation) not found on Aminet.

candidates.name: 9base-ascii
candidates.notes: Research-only. cli utility from 9base project.
candidates.aminet_notes: ascii.lha exists at https://aminet.net/package/util/cli/ascii.lha. Appears to be ASCII utility but verification needed whether it is 9base port.

candidates.name: 9base-hoc
candidates.notes: Research-only. math utility from 9base project.
candidates.aminet_notes: hoc calculator (9base implementation) not found on Aminet. Several Amiga calculator tools exist but not 9base hoc.

candidates.name: sbase-uuencode
candidates.notes: Research-only. archiving utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: UUCode.lha exists at https://www.aminet.net/package/util/arc/UUCode.lha. Handles both encoding and decoding.

candidates.name: sbase-uudecode
candidates.notes: Research-only. archiving utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: UUCode.lha exists at https://www.aminet.net/package/util/arc/UUCode.lha. Handles both encoding and decoding.

candidates.name: sbase-sha256sum
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: sha256.lha exists at https://aminet.net/package/util/cli/sha256. Dedicated SHA256 utility for Amiga. Version/age unknown, needs verification.

candidates.name: sbase-md5sum
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: MD5SUM.lha exists at https://aminet.net/package/util/crypt/MD5SUM. Dedicated MD5 utility for Amiga. Version/age unknown, needs verification.

candidates.name: sbase-split
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: split utility (sbase implementation) not found. Amiga has splitter tools (SimpleSplitter, splitter) but they appear to split archive files, not text files per POSIX split.

candidates.name: sbase-od
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: od (octal dump) utility not found on Aminet. No direct match in search results.

candidates.name: sbase-sha1sum
candidates.notes: Research-only. text_processing utility from sbase project.
candidates.aminet_notes: sha.i386-aros.lha exists at http://aminet.net/package/util/crypt/sha (AROS i386 only, not m68k). No 68k SHA1 tool found on Aminet.

ported.name: tail
ported.description: Display last part of a file

ported.name: patch
ported.description: Apply diff patches to source files

ported.name: less
ported.description: Terminal pager with search and scroll
